history.text: Kievan Rus' appears in Belarus' historical record.
Grand Duchy of Lithuania ruled or strongly influenced the territory before the modern state.
Byelorussian Soviet Socialist Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
A declaration of independence was issued, making sovereignty the central political claim.
Internationally recognised (dissolution of the Soviet Union).
The current constitution came into force, setting the basic framework for today's Belarus.

worthSeeing.title: Bialowieza Forest:
worthSeeing.text: One of Europe's last primeval forests, shared with Poland, home to wild bison.

worthSeeing.title: Mir Castle:
worthSeeing.text: A stunning red-brick UNESCO fortress surrounded by a moat and gardens.

worthSeeing.title: Nesvizh Palace:
worthSeeing.text: A Baroque palace complex and residence of the Radziwill dynasty, UNESCO listed.

planningQuestions.a: Bialowieza Forest, Mir Castle, Nesvizh Palace.
